Purpose/Description: Import Flask framework: Flask, request, render_template, jsonify
Import DatabaseManager class from database_manager module
Import threading (for background tasks) and time (for timestamps)
Import json, os, subprocess, sys for file I/O, OS operations, and process management
Import torisql (Toritani SQL interface), pandas (data manipulation), qr_printer (SATO printer)
Define FALSE_TEST_CSV_PATH - path to CSV fallback database for testing

Create the Flask app object; auto-finds templates/ folder
add_no_cache_headers() - after-request hook prevents browser from caching HTML pages
arduino_signals dict + threading lock - stores pending START/STOP signals from Arduino bridge
server_timers dict + lock - tracks active server-side timers (works when browser is closed)
server_counters dict + lock - tracks kitting counter value per process
server_blocked_counters dict + lock - tracks blocked kitting numbers per process (NG/LINEOUT)
server_active_kittings dict + lock - which process is working on which kitting number
last_data_update dict + lock - timestamp of last data change (for graph auto-refresh)
last_active_date dict + lock - tracks last date system was active (for daily reset detection)
job_order_start_time dict + lock - when current job order started (for graph filtering)
processes_started_today dict + lock - flag to disable manpower warning after first process start
SERVER_START_TIME - epoch timestamp used by browsers to detect server restart and auto-reload
TIMER_STATE_FILE - JSON file path to persist timer state across Flask restarts
LAST_DATE_FILE - JSON file path to persist last active date
JOB_ORDER_FILE - JSON file path to persist job order start time
save_last_active_date() - write last active date string to JSON file
load_last_active_date() - read last active date from JSON file on startup
save_job_order_start_time() - write job order start timestamp to JSON file
load_job_order_start_time() - read job order start timestamp from JSON file on startup
set_new_job_order_start() - set current datetime as new job order start time and persist to file
check_all_processes_same_kitting() - background thread checks if all 9 processes reached same count; triggers graph reset
save_timer_state_to_file() - persist server timers and counters to JSON in background thread
load_timer_state_from_file() - load server timers and counters from JSON on startup; discards stale timers (>2hr)
update_last_data_timestamp() - updates last_data_update timestamp for auto-refresh
check_daily_reset() - detects new day vs last active date; resets all server state, clears manpower, preserves SQL records
clear_all_server_state() - manually clear all timers, counters, Arduino signals, and delete state file
initialize_processes_started_today() - check DB on startup for today's records to set manpower warning flag

KITTING_MATERIAL_CATEGORIES dict - maps 26 row numbers to material category keywords for matching
getQuantityAndSuffix() - POST API to fetch qty and suffix from Toritani SQL or CSV fallback with 3 retries

fix_main_db() - POST API to fix existing MAIN_DB lot_qty data
save_joborder_plan() - POST API to save joborder_plan record when kitting QR scanned

SETTINGS_FILE and KITTING_MATERIALS_FILE paths for persisting material setter settings

CUSTOM_TESTS_FILE path and load/save functions for custom self-test definitions
get_custom_tests() - GET API to return saved custom test definitions from JSON

run_self_test() - POST API to run built-in or custom functional tests (15 built-in test types + custom DB tests)
force_daily_reset() - POST API to manually trigger full daily reset (clear all state and manpower)
start_process() - POST API to handle START button; validates upstream process, starts server timer, increments counter
stop_process() - POST API to handle STOP button; calculates elapsed time, inserts record to DB, clears timer
ng_process() - POST API to handle NG button; validates upstream, inserts record with pass_ng=0, blocks counter
ng_lineout_process() - POST API to handle NG LINEOUT; inserts record with lineout_reason and pass_ng=0
lineout_process() - POST API to handle LINEOUT; inserts record with lineout_reason
get_process_records() - GET API to get process records for a specific process
get_standard_times() - GET API to get all standard times
get_standard_time() - GET API to get standard time for a specific process
update_standard_time() - POST API to update standard time and title for a process
add_process() - POST API to add a new process with standard time
delete_process() - POST API to delete a process (only processes > 9)
check_manpower_complete() - GET API to check if all processes have operators assigned
get_manpower() - GET API to get all manpower records
get_manpower_by_process() - GET API to get manpower for a specific process
get_cycle_graph_data() - GET API to get average elapsed time per process for bar chart
get_line_trend_data() - GET API to get per-kitting elapsed time records for line trend chart (today only, with job order reset filter)
update_manpower() - POST API to update operator data (manual entry or scan)
operator_out() - POST API to handle operator OUT (clear manpower, record OUT reason, update time_out)
get_out_reasons() - GET API to get all OUT reasons from bio_break table

delete_out_reason() - POST API to delete an OUT reason
get_lineout_reasons() - GET API to get all LINE OUT reasons
add_lineout_reason() - POST API to add a custom LINE OUT reason
delete_lineout_reason() - POST API to delete a LINE OUT reason
in_line_process() - POST API to handle IN-LINE action (resume after lineout with in_line_reason and repaired_action)

delete_in_line_reason() - POST API to delete an IN-LINE reason
get_repaired_actions() - GET API to get all REPAIRED ACTION reasons
add_repaired_action() - POST API to add a custom REPAIRED ACTION reason
delete_repaired_action() - POST API to delete a REPAIRED ACTION reason
stop_repaired_process() - POST API to stop a repaired process (same as stop_process but with repaired flag)
receive_arduino_signal() - POST API to receive Arduino serial signals; handles server-side timer start/stop with manpower and upstream validation
get_arduino_signal() - GET API for browser polling of pending Arduino signals
get_server_timer() - GET API to get current server-side timer state for a process
sync_counter() - POST API to sync kitting counter value between browser and server
clear_server_timer() - POST API to clear server-side timer for a process
start_server_timer() - POST API to start server-side timer for a process
stop_server_timer() - POST API to stop server-side timer for a process
get_completed_count() - GET API to get completed record count for a process today
can_start_kitting() - GET API to check if a process can start (upstream validation)
get_all_active_timers() - GET API to get all currently active server timers
get_last_update() - GET API to get timestamp of last data update (for auto-refresh)
get_server_start_time() - GET API to get server start timestamp (for auto-reload detection)
block_counter() / is_counter_blocked() - POST/GET APIs to manage blocked kitting counters
get_blocked_counters() / clear_blocked_counters() - GET/POST APIs to retrieve and clear blocked counters
set_active_kitting() / clear_active_kitting() - POST APIs to manage active kitting assignments per process
is_kitting_active() / get_all_active_kittings() - GET APIs to check kitting status

print_kitting_qr() - POST API to print QR code label to SATO PW208NX printer; api_list_printers() - GET API to list available printers
